{{draft categories| [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Scenic Spots in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nanjō, Okinawa]] [[Category:History of Nanjō, Okinawa]] [[Category:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]] [[Category:Ryukyu Kingdom]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ryukyu Islands]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated scenic spots]] [[Category:Religious places]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:斎場御嶽]] [[en:Sefa-utaki]] [[sn:Sefa-utaki]]

{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
```plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of {{ill|Kudaka Island|ja|久高島|12=draft}} from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a {{ill|Historic Site (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} located in {{ill|Nanjō, Okinawa|ja|南城市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chinen, Okinawa|ja|知念|12=draft}} in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an {{ill|Utaki|ja|御嶽 (沖縄)|12=draft}} from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=draft}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=draft}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=draft}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view {{ill|Kudaka Island|ja|久高島|12=draft}}, considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-ranking priestess, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|ja|聞得大君|12=draft}}. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "{{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|ja|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群|12=draft}}." It is also designated a National {{ill|Historic Site (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of {{ill|Ferns|ja|シダ植物|12=draft}} and {{ill|Orchidaceae|ja|ラン科|12=draft}}. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, {{ill|Nanjō, Okinawa|ja|南城市|12=draft}} authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for {{ill|Theft (crime)|ja|窃盗罪|12=draft}}, and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
```plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of {{ill|Kudaka Island|ja|久高島|12=draft}} from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a {{ill|Historic Site (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} located in {{ill|Nanjō, Okinawa|ja|南城市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chinen, Okinawa|ja|知念|12=draft}} in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an {{ill|Utaki|ja|御嶽 (沖縄)|12=draft}} from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=draft}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=draft}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=draft}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view {{ill|Kudaka Island|ja|久高島|12=draft}}, considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-ranking priestess, the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|ja|聞得大君|12=draft}}. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "{{ill|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|ja|琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群|12=draft}}." It is also designated a National {{ill|Historic Site (Japan)|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of {{ill|Ferns|ja|シダ植物|12=draft}} and {{ill|Orchidaceae|ja|ラン科|12=draft}}. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, {{ill|Nanjō, Okinawa|ja|南城市|12=draft}} authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for {{ill|Theft (crime)|ja|窃盗罪|12=draft}}, and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

{{Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu}} {{coord|26|10|24|N|127|49|36|E|display=title|region:JP-47_type:landmark}} {{history-stub}}

*[[George H. Kerr|Kerr]], George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945.''] Washington, D.C.: Pacific Science Board, National Academy of Sciences, National Research Council. {{OCLC|5455582}} * Yamakage, Motohisa, Paul de Leeuw and Aidan Rankin. (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&q=Seifa-utaki ''The essence of Shinto.''] Tokyo: Kodansha International. {{ISBN|978-4-7700-3044-3}}
{{Commons category|Sefautaki}} {{Authority control}} {{Coord|26|10|24|N|127|49|36|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Religious-struct-stub}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Scenic Spots in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nanjō, Okinawa]] [[Category:History of Nanjō, Okinawa]] [[Category:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]] [[Category:Ryukyu Kingdom]] [[Category:Ryukyu Islands]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated scenic spots]] [[Category:Religious places]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:斎場御嶽]] [[en:Sefa-utaki]] [[sn:Sefa-utaki]]

{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年1月14日 (火) 00:33 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄せいふあうたき}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|斎場御嶽、三庫理(さんぐぅい)]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|三庫理から眺む[[久高島]]]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=斎場御嶽|width=256}} '''斎場御嶽'''（せーふぁーうたき<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |title = ロケ候補地詳細 |publisher = 文化庁 |accessdate = 2018-08-13 }}</ref>／サイハノうたき）は[[沖縄県]][[南城市]][[知念村|知念]]にある[[史跡]]。[[15世紀]]-[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]であるとされる。「せーふぁ」は「最高位」を意味し、「斎場御嶽」は「最高の御嶽」ほどの意味となり、これは通称である。正式な神名は「'''君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ'''」という。 == 施設と伝承 == 敷地内には首里城内の施設名と同じ拝所が複数ある。 3つの拝所が集中する最奥部の三庫理（さんぐーい）には「チョウノハナ（京の華）」という最も格の高い拝所があり、クバの木を伝って琉球の創世神であるアマミクが降臨するとされる。
なお、三庫理からは王国開闢にまつわる最高聖地とされている[[久高島]]を遥拝することができるが、これについては史書には記述がない。これは、近世になって三庫理の岩壁の一角が崩れたことによるもので、かつての三庫理は三方を岩壁に囲まれた空間だった。なお、三庫理内部が立入禁止になったため、現在は久高島遥拝所も立ち入ることができない。
== 王国時代の斎場御嶽 == 文字通り王国最高の御嶽とされ、国家の最高神職である[[聞得大君]]が管理した。聞得大君の就任儀式「御新下り（おあらおり）」が行われた御嶽でもある。かつて琉球の御嶽はその全てが[[男子禁制]]であり、斎場御嶽では庶民は入口の御門口（うじょーぐち）を越えて進入することは許されず、国王であっても、御門口より先に入るには袂の合わせを女装に改める必要があったという。
== 文化的価値 == [[2000年]]12月、[[首里城]]跡などとともに「[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]」として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）に登録された。また、国の[[史跡]]、沖縄県指定名勝にも指定されており、御嶽内で出土した陶磁器と勾玉などは「沖縄県斎場御嶽出土品」の名称で国の[[重要文化財]]（考古資料）に指定されている。
周囲の森林は沖縄本島南部におけるもっとも優れた森林の一つで、[[シダ植物]]や[[ラン科]]植物などに珍しいものが多い。本島中南部は第二次世界大戦において被害を受けそれ以前の状態を残した場所がほとんどないが、この地域は戦災を免れた。しかし、いくつかの艦砲弾着弾跡があり、一つは見学道から見ることができる。2000年代以降御嶽周辺が整備され、森林に荒廃の様子が見られる。
== 観光地として == {{節スタブ|date=2025年2月}} 2007年7月1日より観覧が有料化された。
== その他 == 訪れる観光客の大幅増加により、[[石畳]]がすり減るようになったり、祈っている地元住民を無断で写真撮影するなどするマナー違反の観光客が増加したりしており問題となっている。このため地元の南城市では文化財保護を図るため、将来は男子禁制を徹底させることも検討している<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |title=「男子禁制」徹底も 沖縄、世界遺産の斎場御嶽|publisher= [[産経新聞]] |date=2013-09-20|archiveurl=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archivedate=2014-01-14}}</ref>。
[[2021年]][[4月5日]]、南城市は斎場御嶽の15基の[[香炉]]のうち、1基が行方がなくなり、別の[[石材]]に置き換えられたと発表した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|website=ryukyushimpo.jp|accessdate=2021-04-07|title=沖縄の世界遺産「斎場御嶽」で盗難か 香炉が別の石材に置き換えられる|publisher=[[琉球新報]]|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>。犯人は[[窃盗罪|窃盗容疑]]で逮捕され香炉も無事戻ってきたが、以前は内部にまで入ることができた三庫理が、立入禁止地域に設定されるなどの影響があった。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[斎場御嶽]] to [[Sefa-utaki]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年1月14日 (火) 00:33 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄せいふあうたき}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|斎場御嶽、三庫理(さんぐぅい)]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|三庫理から眺む[[久高島]]]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=斎場御嶽|width=256}} '''斎場御嶽'''（せーふぁーうたき<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url = https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |title = ロケ候補地詳細 |publisher = 文化庁 |accessdate = 2018-08-13 }}</ref>／サイハノうたき）は[[沖縄県]][[南城市]][[知念村|知念]]にある[[史跡]]。[[15世紀]]-[[16世紀]]の[[琉球王国]]・[[尚真]]王時代の[[御嶽 (沖縄)|御嶽]]であるとされる。「せーふぁ」は「最高位」を意味し、「斎場御嶽」は「最高の御嶽」ほどの意味となり、これは通称である。正式な神名は「'''君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ'''」という。 == 施設と伝承 == 敷地内には首里城内の施設名と同じ拝所が複数ある。 3つの拝所が集中する最奥部の三庫理（さんぐーい）には「チョウノハナ（京の華）」という最も格の高い拝所があり、クバの木を伝って琉球の創世神であるアマミクが降臨するとされる。
なお、三庫理からは王国開闢にまつわる最高聖地とされている[[久高島]]を遥拝することができるが、これについては史書には記述がない。これは、近世になって三庫理の岩壁の一角が崩れたことによるもので、かつての三庫理は三方を岩壁に囲まれた空間だった。なお、三庫理内部が立入禁止になったため、現在は久高島遥拝所も立ち入ることができない。
== 王国時代の斎場御嶽 == 文字通り王国最高の御嶽とされ、国家の最高神職である[[聞得大君]]が管理した。聞得大君の就任儀式「御新下り（おあらおり）」が行われた御嶽でもある。かつて琉球の御嶽はその全てが[[男子禁制]]であり、斎場御嶽では庶民は入口の御門口（うじょーぐち）を越えて進入することは許されず、国王であっても、御門口より先に入るには袂の合わせを女装に改める必要があったという。
== 文化的価値 == [[2000年]]12月、[[首里城]]跡などとともに「[[琉球王国のグスク及び関連遺産群]]」として[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]]の[[世界遺産]]（文化遺産）に登録された。また、国の[[史跡]]、沖縄県指定名勝にも指定されており、御嶽内で出土した陶磁器と勾玉などは「沖縄県斎場御嶽出土品」の名称で国の[[重要文化財]]（考古資料）に指定されている。
周囲の森林は沖縄本島南部におけるもっとも優れた森林の一つで、[[シダ植物]]や[[ラン科]]植物などに珍しいものが多い。本島中南部は第二次世界大戦において被害を受けそれ以前の状態を残した場所がほとんどないが、この地域は戦災を免れた。しかし、いくつかの艦砲弾着弾跡があり、一つは見学道から見ることができる。2000年代以降御嶽周辺が整備され、森林に荒廃の様子が見られる。
== 観光地として == {{節スタブ|date=2025年2月}} 2007年7月1日より観覧が有料化された。
== その他 == 訪れる観光客の大幅増加により、[[石畳]]がすり減るようになったり、祈っている地元住民を無断で写真撮影するなどするマナー違反の観光客が増加したりしており問題となっている。このため地元の南城市では文化財保護を図るため、将来は男子禁制を徹底させることも検討している<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |title=「男子禁制」徹底も 沖縄、世界遺産の斎場御嶽|publisher= [[産経新聞]] |date=2013-09-20|archiveurl=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archivedate=2014-01-14}}</ref>。
[[2021年]][[4月5日]]、南城市は斎場御嶽の15基の[[香炉]]のうち、1基が行方がなくなり、別の[[石材]]に置き換えられたと発表した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|website=ryukyushimpo.jp|accessdate=2021-04-07|title=沖縄の世界遺産「斎場御嶽」で盗難か 香炉が別の石材に置き換えられる|publisher=[[琉球新報]]|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>。犯人は[[窃盗罪|窃盗容疑]]で逮捕され香炉も無事戻ってきたが、以前は内部にまで入ることができた三庫理が、立入禁止地域に設定されるなどの影響があった。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

(2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==

Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.

Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.

It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[[sn:Sefa-utaki]] [[ja:斎場御嶽]] [[en:Sefa-utaki]] [[Category:南城市の建築物]] [[Category:南城市の歴史]] [[Category:御嶽 (沖縄)]] [[Category:National historic sites in Okinawa prefecture]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

{{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto Shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[World War II]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>
