The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==

Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. == As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] {{wikidata link|Q3087076}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].

[[ja:斎場御嶽]] [[en:Sefa-utaki]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanjo City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nanjō]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:National historic sites in Okinawa prefecture]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] {{wikidata link|Q3087076}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>

plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).

At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).

Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>

[https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh_min_nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh_min_nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>

plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh_min_nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh_min_nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).

Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>

The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

[[ja:斎場御嶽]] [[en:Sefa-utaki]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cs:Seifa-utaki]] [[fr:Seifa-utaki]] [[id:Sefa Utaki]] [[it:Sefa-utaki]] [[vi:Seifa-utaki]] [[zh:齋場御嶽]] [[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]] {{wikidata link|Q3087076}} [[Category:Architecture of Nanjo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nanjō]] [[Category:National historic sites in Okinawa prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated scenic spots]] [[Category:Religious places]] [[Category:Ryukyu Islands]] [[Category:Ryukyu Kingdom]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Utaki (Okinawa)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sefautaki]][[cs:Seifa-utaki]][[de:Seifa Utaki]][[en:Sefa-utaki]][[fr:Seifa-utaki]][[id:Sefa Utaki]][[ja:斎場御嶽]][[tr:Sefa-utaki]][[vi:Seifa-utaki]][[zh-min-nan:Sefa Utaki]][[zh:齋場御嶽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3087076]] Wikidata name: Sefa-utaki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Sefa-utaki |image=Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg }} [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|thumb|250px|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sefa-utaki]] {{Nihongo|'''Sefa-utaki'''|斎場御嶽||extra={{langx|ryu|シェーファウタキ}} ''Seefa-utaki''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://ryukyu-lang.lib.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/srnh/details.php?ID=SN50769|title=シェーファウタキ|website=首里・那覇方言データベース}}</ref>}}, meaning "purified place of [[Utaki]],"<ref>Yamakage, Motohisa ''et al.'' (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=cfS8RWn3y58C&dq=Seifa-utaki&pg=PA69 ''The essence of Shinto,'' p. 69.]</ref> is a historical sacred space, overlooking [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], that served as one of the key locations of worship in the [[Ryukyuan religion|native religion]] of the [[:en:Ryukyuan people|Ryukyuan people]] for millennia.<ref name="unesco">{{cite web |title=Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/154726 |website=UNESCO |access-date=1 April 2024}}</ref> Later as a part of assimilation of Okinawa by Japan, it was shifted to serve as a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. It is part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] ''[[Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]'' in [[:en:Nanjō, Okinawa|Nanjō]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]].
Sefa Utaki is on the Chinen Peninsula, and has been recognized as a sacred place since the earliest period of Ryukyuan history. According to ''Chūzan Seikan'', this was the spot where [[Amamikyu]], goddess of creation, made landfall on Okinawa. The shrine area itself comprises a number of caves and overhanging ledges opening to the east and south among towering rock formations of a high promontory over the sea. All buildings have been destroyed, but the outer and inner precincts can still be traced.<ref>Kerr, George H. (1953). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Q5IrAAAAYAAJ&q=Seifa-utaki ''Ryukyu Kingdom and Province before 1945,'' p. 10.]</ref>
==Overview==
plaintext {{Source|date=January 14, 2014 (Tue) 00:33 (UTC)|sortkey=Okinawa Sefa-utaki}} [[File:Sangui (Triangle Rock) of Sefa-Utaki.jpg|200px|right|thumb|Sefa-utaki, Sangui]] [[File:Kudakajima Island from Sangui of Sefa-Utaki 1.JPG|200px|right|thumb|View of [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]] from Sangui]] {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{coord|26.173333|127.826667}}|caption=Sefa-utaki|width=256}} '''Sefa-utaki''' (斎場御嶽, '''Sēfa-utaki''')<ref>{{Cite web|title=Location details |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |url=https://www.jldb.bunka.go.jp/creator/locations/detail/470100112/ |accessdate=2018-08-13}}</ref> is a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} located in [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]], [[:en:Chinen|Chinen, Okinawa]] in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It is believed to be an [[:en:Utaki|Utaki]] from the period of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] under King {{ill|Shō Shin|ja|尚真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尚真王|1=Shō Shin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q281765}} (15th-16th century). The term "Sēfa" means "highest Rank," and thus "Sefa-utaki" translates to "the highest sacred site." The formal sacred name is '''Kimi-ga-dake, Nushi-ga-dake-no-ibi''' (君ガ嶽、主ガ嶽ノイビ).
== Facilities and Legends == The site contains multiple places of worship sharing names with locations within [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]]. In the innermost area, known as Sangui (三庫理), three {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship sites|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} converge. Among them, "Chōnohana (京の華)" is the highest-Ranking site, where it is said that the Ryukyuan creation deity {{ill|Amamikyu|ja|アマミク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamikyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} descended through a kuba (fan palm) tree.
From Sangui, visitors can view [[:en:Kudaka Island|Kudaka Island]], considered the holiest site associated with the kingdom's creation. However, historical documents do not mention this view, as a section of the rock wall collapsed in modern times. Originally, Sangui was enclosed on three sides by rock walls. Since access to the inner Sangui area was prohibited, visitors can no longer access the Kudaka Island viewing point.
== Sefa-utaki during the Ryukyu Kingdom Era == Sefa-utaki was regarded as the highest sacred site in the kingdom and was under the administration of the highest-Ranking priestess, the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]]. It was the site of the priestess’s inauguration ceremony known as "Oaraori" (御新下り). In the Ryukyu Kingdom, all utaki were traditionally off-limits to men. At Sefa-utaki, commoners were not permitted beyond the outer gate (Ujōguchi, 御門口), and even the king had to dress in women’s attire to enter beyond the gate.
== Cultural Significance == In December 2000, Sefa-utaki, along with [[:en:Shuri Castle|Shuri Castle]] and other related sites, was inscribed on the [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage List]] as part of the "[[:en:Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu|Gusuku Sites and Related Properties of the Kingdom of Ryukyu]]." It is also designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} and a Prefectural Scenic Spot by Okinawa Prefecture. Additionally, artifacts such as ceramics and magatama beads unearthed at Sefa-utaki have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (archaeological materials).
The surrounding forest is considered one of the finest in southern Okinawa Island, with rare species of [[:en:Pteridophyte|Ferns]] and [[:en:Orchid|Orchidaceae]]. Although most of southern Okinawa was devastated during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], this area escaped major destruction. However, several naval artillery shell impact craters are visible, including one along the visitor path. Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>

Since the 2000s, conservation work has been undertaken, but signs of forest degradation have appeared.
== As a Tourist Site == {{stub-section|date=February 2025}} Since July 1, 2007, an admission fee has been charged for visitors.
== Issues and Challenges == With a significant increase in visitors, issues such as the wearing down of the stone-paved paths and tourists photographing local worshippers without permission have arisen. In response, [[:en:Nanjō|Nanjō, Okinawa]] authorities are considering reinstating a male-entry prohibition to protect the cultural heritage.<ref>{{Cite news|title="Reinforcing male-entry prohibition considered for UNESCO World Heritage Site Sefa-utaki"|publisher=[[:en:Sankei Shimbun|Sankei Shimbun]]|url=http://sankei.jp.msn.com/politics/news/130920/lcl13092020290001-n1.htm |date=2013-09-20|archive-url=https://archive.is/6BHI0|archive-date=2014-01-14}}</ref>
On April 5, 2021, Nanjō City reported the disappearance of one of the 15 incense burners (kōro, 香炉) from the site, which was replaced by a different stone. The perpetrator was later arrested for [[:en:Larceny|Theft (crime)]], and the stolen item was recovered. However, due to this incident, access to the Sangui area was restricted.<ref>{{Cite web|title="Possible theft at Okinawa World Heritage Sefa-utaki: Incense burner replaced by another stone"|publisher=[[Ryukyu Shimpo]]|url=https://ryukyushimpo.jp/news/entry-1299383.html|accessdate=2021-04-07|date=2021-04-06}}</ref>
== References ==
